It looks like there is a simpler version of Process Lasso called "Coredirector", also from the same developer which should allow us to achieve the same goal, keep the evadus main app and evadus+ off any E-Cores.

It's very important that the main app as well as the VM doesn't run on any E-Cores, they are connected to each other.

Ok yeah that makes sense that you didn't find anything, I found this:

Thats what I did

Here it is:

Install and run Process Lasso.
Navigate to Options > CPU > CPU Affinities.
Create a new rule for the process name qemu-system-x86_64.exe.
In the affinity selection dialog, manually select the checkboxes corresponding to the P-cores.
Save the rule.

if you need help let me know

alright so this is a pretty simple problem.

These days, most Intel CPU's come with "E" (effeciency) and "P" (performance) cores.

THe P cores are high performance, meant for doing intense stuff, whilst the E-cores are really fucking weak, but they draw little power and are good for background tasks.

The problem is that your machine is assigning the E-cores to the VM, making it piss slow.

The easiest fix is a tool called "Process Lasso", which can identify and allocate P-cores and E-Core, whilst the most aggressive approach is simply disabling all the E-Cores in the BIOS.

I think we should try the Process Lasso method first
